---
title: "cheatsheet"
output: html_document
---
##Cheat Sheet Contains Information from Lab 6-Lab 15

continuous varibales: use scatter plot 
categorical variables: use bar plot
- use facet_wrap() to create multiple plots based on a categorical variable



##Lab 6:  
Functions:
- distinct(): find unique observations in rows (sees if there are multiple rows)
- mutate(): create new variables as columns 
- mutate() WITH across() and where() to transform multiple columns that meet specific criteria
    ex: penguins %>% 
          mutate(across(ends_with("mm"), ~./10)) %>% 

# ~ means "use this function" > replaced existing columns with "mm" converting them to cm (doesn't change the names)
#What does the `~./10` mean? The `~` indicates that what follows is a formula (lambda function). 
#The `.` represents the current column being processed. So, `./10` means "take the current column and divide it by 10". This operation is applied to all columns that end with `mm`.

select(species,
         bill_length_cm=bill_length_mm,
         bill_depth_cm=bill_depth_mm,
         flipper_length_cm=flipper_length_mm)
         
         
- group_by() and summarise(): to calculate summary statistics for groups of data
- use if_else() to conditionally change values in a column
- %in% operator to filter data based on multiple values in a column
      ex: penguins%>% filter(species %in% c("Adelie", "Chinstrap"))

#HW 6:
#uses mutate to change the variables to factors
- gabon <- gabon %>%
  mutate(across(c(hunt_cat, land_use, transect_id), as.factor)) 
#uses $ to show the number of transects for each land use type
- table(gabon$land_use)
# For which land use type (national parks, logging, or neither) is average all species diversity the greatest?**

gabon %>% filter(land_use=="park") -> parks
gabon %>% filter(land_use=="logging") -> logging
gabon %>% filter(land_use=="neither") -> neither
mean(parks$diversity_all_species)
mean(logging$diversity_all_species)
mean(neither$diversity_all_species)


##Lab 7:
Functions:
- summarize(): to calculate summary statistics for a data frame
- count(): to count the number of occurrences of a specific value in a column


##Lab 9:
Rules:  
+ `pivot_longer`(cols, names_to, values_to)
+ `cols` - Columns to pivot to longer format
+ `names_to` - Name of the new column; it will contain the column names of gathered columns as values
+ `values_to` - Name of the new column; it will contain the data stored in the values of gathered columns
- use `pivot_longer()` when our column names actually represent variables. A classic example would be that the column names represent observations of a variable







## Visualization with ggplot2 (Labs 9-12)
Lab 9: Basics

ggplot(data, aes(x, y)) + geom_point() (scatter) or geom_line().

Lab 10: Distributions

geom_bar() (counts rows), geom_col() (uses specific y-value), geom_histogram() (binned continuous data).

Lab 11: Comparison & Style

geom_boxplot(), labs(title=, x=, y=), theme() (adjusting text/angles), fill= vs color=.

Lab 12: Advanced Layouts

facet_wrap(~variable) (wrapped grid), facet_grid(y~x) (matrix grid), scaling within facets.

## Apps and AI (Labs 13-15)
Lab 13: Shiny Basics

ui <- fluidPage(), server <- function(input, output), shinyApp(ui, server).

Inputs: selectInput(), sliderInput(), radioButtons().

Lab 14: Reactive Shiny

renderPlot(), plotOutput(), reactive({}) (for creating filtered dataframes that update based on user input).

Lab 15: AI & Projects

Prompt engineering for R (using AI to debug errors), final project organization, and README.md structure.

💡 Quick Tips for the Exam:
The Pipe (%>% or |>): Remember that the output of one function becomes the first argument of the next.

Aesthetics: aes() is for mapping variables to the plot; anything constant (like color = "blue") goes outside aes().

NAs: If your mean returns NA, you forgot na.rm = TRUE.

    Lab,Primary Goal,Key Functions to Know
Lab 6,Summarizing,"group_by(), summarize(), n(), count(), mean(na.rm=T), sum(), median()"
Lab 7,Reshaping,"pivot_longer(), pivot_wider(), separate(), unite()"
Lab 8,NAs,"is.na(), n_miss(), miss_var_summary(), replace_with_na(), na.omit()"
Lab 9,GGPlot 1,"ggplot(aes(x, y)), geom_point(), geom_line(), geom_smooth()"
Lab 10,GGPlot 2,"geom_bar() (counts), geom_col() (values), geom_histogram(), fill="
Lab 11,GGPlot 3,"geom_boxplot(), labs(title=, x=, y=), theme_minimal(), theme(axis.text.x=)"
Lab 12,Faceting,"facet_wrap(~var), facet_grid(row~col), scales = ""free"""
Lab 13,Shiny UI,"fluidPage(), selectInput(), plotOutput(), numericInput()"
Lab 14,Shiny Server,"renderPlot(), reactive({}), input$ID, session"
Lab 15,AI/Workflows,"names(), glimpse(), summary(), str()
